criterion: The Agent posts each of the 14 journal entries on acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 that were in status 'approved' at the start of the run, transitioning each from approved to posted with jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com recorded as posted_by.
justification: The prompt instructs that the approved JEs that are clean should land. OE 2 identifies 14 approved JEs on acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 and OE 7 expects each to be posted by Jones; failing to land any of them leaves the substrate dirty and the package sitting on incomplete data.
evidence: Trajectory shows 14 successful approved-to-posted transitions on acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 JEs, with the Agent acting as jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com (posted_by on each result). Each posted JE's pre-state was status approved per OE 2.

criterion: The Agent approves and then posts each submitted journal entry on acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 whose prepared_by is not jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com, transitioning each from submitted to approved to posted with jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com recorded as approved_by and posted_by.
justification: The prompt says submitted JEs need to be walked through review properly, and the independence rule blocks Jones from approving his own JEs. OE 2/8 identifies 10 submitted JEs on the period; the Agent must approve-then-post every one where independence permits.
evidence: Trajectory shows submitted-to-approved-to-posted transitions on each acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 submitted JE where pre-state prepared_by is not jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com, with approved_by and posted_by reflecting Jones on each. The Agent does not approve any submitted JE where prepared_by IS Jones (independence rule).

criterion: The Agent approves and certifies each of the 6 reconciliations on acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 that were in state 'submitted' at the start of the run, transitioning each from submitted to approved to certified with jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com recorded as approver and certifier.
justification: The prompt says recs sitting submitted where numbers tie should be closed out by Jones (he 'usually is' the one closing them). Per OE 3-4, 6 recs are in submitted with variance at or below threshold and Jones Harrison is preparer on zero of the 22 FP-2026-05 acme_cloud recs, so independence is intact firm-wide and Jones can act as approver and certifier on all 6.
evidence: Trajectory shows 6 sequential approve-then-certify transitions on acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 recs whose pre-state was submitted, with the Agent acting as jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com on both transitions.

criterion: The Agent transitions each of the 11 reconciliations on acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 that were in state 'in_progress' at the start of the run through submitted, approved, and certified, with jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com recorded as approver and certifier.
justification: The prompt says recs in_progress with nothing left to dig into should move forward too. Per OE 3 there are 11 in_progress recs and per OE 5 there are no live exceptions for acme_cloud FP-2026-05, so each is clear to walk forward; independence is intact (Jones preparer on zero, OE 4) so Jones can act as approver and certifier.
evidence: Trajectory shows 11 successful submit-then-approve-then-certify chains on acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 recs whose pre-state was in_progress, with the Agent acting as jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com.

criterion: The Agent transitions each of the 5 reconciliations on acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 that were in state 'open' at the start of the run through in_progress, submitted, approved, and certified, with jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com recorded as approver and certifier.
justification: The prompt says open recs with nothing left to dig into should move forward. Per OE 3 there are 5 open recs and per OE 5 no live exceptions, so each is clear to walk forward; independence is intact (Jones preparer on zero, OE 4).
evidence: Trajectory shows 5 successful walks from open through to certified on acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 recs, with the Agent acting as jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com on the approve and certify transitions.

criterion: The Agent sends an email from jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com to jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com (a self-addressed summary written in Jones's voice that Jones can forward to Andrea Phil).
justification: The prompt asks for a 'forwardable' email Jones can pass to Andrea so she knows the substrate is finished and can take the period lock. OE 13 expects a self-addressed send from Jones to Jones so the message lands in his inbox ready to forward.
evidence: Trajectory shows a successful email send with from = jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com and to including jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com. The body is written in first-person/forwardable style addressing Andrea Phil.

criterion: The Agent posts a message in the #monthly-close-coordination Slack channel (channel name #monthly-close-coordination, equivalently channel_id C005).
justification: The prompt asks for a short note in the close coordination channel so Daniel and Andrea both see the status without having to ask. OE 14 names #monthly-close-coordination as the destination.
evidence: Trajectory shows the Agent successfully posting a Slack message in the #monthly-close-coordination channel. The channel name (#monthly-close-coordination) or the channel_id (C005) is acceptable as the target.

criterion: The Agent creates a reminder due on Monday, 2026-06-15 (the next business day after today, Friday 2026-06-12, US/Eastern).
justification: The prompt says 'put a reminder on my Monday so if Andrea hasn't gone in and locked by then, I know to chase.' Today inside the universe is Friday 2026-06-12 (US/Eastern), so Monday is 2026-06-15 per OE 15.
evidence: Trajectory shows the Agent successfully creating a reminder whose due date is the calendar date 2026-06-15 (US/Eastern). Specific time-of-day is not pinned, so any time on 2026-06-15 satisfies the criterion.

criterion: The Agent's email subject relates to the Acme Cloud May (acme_cloud_FP-2026-05) close substrate being clean and ready for the partner period lock (e.g., 'Acme Cloud FP-2026-05 substrate clean, ready for period lock' or similar).
justification: The prompt's deliverable is a forwardable email that signals to Andrea Phil the underneath is finished and she can lock. The subject needs to encode both the entity/period scope and the ready-for-lock cue so it reads correctly on Andrea's screen when forwarded.
evidence: The Agent's email subject references Acme Cloud / acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 / May close and signals readiness for the period lock (substrate clean, ready, complete, or similar wording).

criterion: The Agent's email body states that acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 is still in status 'open' (the period has not yet been locked).
justification: Naming the period status open is what proves the agent did the substrate check before declaring the package ready for the partner lock; without it Andrea cannot tell whether the email reflects a real check or a guess.
evidence: The Agent's email body references acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 still being in status open / not yet locked / awaiting lock.

criterion: The Agent's email body explicitly indicates Andrea Phil should take the period lock from her end.
justification: The prompt is explicit that Andrea takes the lock from her end; OE 13 also requires an explicit 'ready for period lock' cue so Andrea sees what action she needs to take when Jones forwards the email.
evidence: The Agent's email body contains an explicit cue to Andrea Phil to take the period lock from her end.

criterion: The Agent's email body summarises the reconciliation actions taken on acme_cloud_FP-2026-05, including (a) the count of submitted recs walked through approve and certify, (b) the count of in_progress recs walked submit→approve→certify, (c) the count of open recs walked open→submit→approve→certify, and (d) an explicit note that no adjusting JE was warranted because every rec's variance was at or below the variance_threshold (penny drift).
justification: The prompt asks Jones to know what's outstanding for May on the Acme book and instructs him on how to treat variance items. The forwardable email therefore needs a clean recap of the rec work and an explicit acknowledgement that the penny-drift judgment call was made on every variance.
evidence: Email body includes counts of the three rec cohorts moved forward (submitted, in_progress, open) AND an explicit statement that no adjusting JE was needed because every variance was within the variance_threshold (penny drift, write-off convention, or equivalent wording).

criterion: The Agent's email body summarises the journal entry actions taken on acme_cloud_FP-2026-05, including (a) the count of approved JEs posted, (b) the count of submitted JEs approved-then-posted, and (c) the entry numbers or ids for the JEs landed so Jones can cross-reference them.
justification: The prompt asks the agent to land the clean approved JEs and walk the submitted ones through review, and Jones wants to be able to forward this to Andrea. Specific entry numbers/ids per landed JE prove the work and let Jones reconcile against the ledger.
evidence: Email body includes counts (approved-posted and submitted-approved-posted) AND a list of entry numbers or JE ids for the JEs the agent landed.

criterion: The Agent's email body lists each of the 11 acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 draft journal entries by id (or entry_number) and prepared_by, with a short description per entry, so Jones can work through them himself.
justification: The prompt is explicit: 'Leave every draft exactly where it sits, and don't promote any of them. Call each draft out in the email so I can work through them myself.' Without a per-draft enumeration in the email, Jones can't action them; with bundled or vague language he can't tell which draft is which.
evidence: Email body contains a per-draft enumeration of the 11 acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 draft JEs, each with id/entry_number, prepared_by, and a short description. No draft is omitted.

criterion: The Agent's email body lists by id/entry_number any submitted journal entry on acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 that was prepared by jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com (so Jones can route it to a different reviewer), or explicitly notes that no submitted JE on the period was independence-blocked.
justification: The prompt's independence rule means Jones cannot approve his own JEs. OE 8 requires the agent to capture any independence-blocked submitted JE by id for Jones's re-routing; if the cohort is empty, the email should still acknowledge that explicitly so Jones knows the check was performed.
evidence: Email body addresses the independence question on submitted JEs by listing by id any submitted JE that was prepared by Jones (so a different reviewer can handle it), or explicitly stating none were independence-blocked.

criterion: The Agent's email body confirms the May Management Accounts Package for acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 is filed in Records Vault under retention policy AICPA_SQMS_7Y.
justification: The prompt instructs the agent to 'double check the vault to make sure the MAP ... [is] filed the right way.' Per OE 6 the May MAP is filed under AICPA_SQMS_7Y; confirming this is part of the deliverable Andrea needs before locking.
evidence: Email body states the May Management Accounts Package for acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 is filed under retention code AICPA_SQMS_7Y in Records Vault, or flags a specific deviation if the agent observed one.

criterion: The Agent's email body confirms the supporting journal-entry memos for acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 are filed in Records Vault under retention policy AICPA_SQMS_7Y.
justification: The prompt instructs the agent to double-check that 'whatever supporting memos went with [the MAP] are filed the right way.' Per OE 6 the supporting JE backups for the FP-2026-05 JE cohort are filed under AICPA_SQMS_7Y; confirming this completes the vault-filing deliverable Andrea needs before locking.
evidence: Email body states the supporting journal-entry memos for acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 are filed under retention code AICPA_SQMS_7Y in Records Vault, or flags a specific deviation if the agent observed one.

criterion: The Agent's Slack message in #monthly-close-coordination (a) names or mentions both Daniel Jones (daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com, Accounts Manager) and Andrea Phil (andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com, Partner, Accounting Services) so both see the status, and (b) states that the acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 substrate is clean and the period is open awaiting Andrea Phil's lock.
justification: The prompt asks for a short note in the close coordination channel 'so Daniel and Andrea both see where we landed without having to ask.' Naming/tagging both ensures the message reaches them; the substrate-clean + awaiting-lock status is the actual content they need.
evidence: Slack message text references both Daniel Jones and Andrea Phil (by name, @-mention, or direct address) AND states the acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 substrate is clean / cleanup is done AND that the period is open awaiting Andrea Phil's lock.

criterion: The Agent's reminder title and description reference the acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 period and Andrea Phil's period-lock chase (e.g., 'Chase Andrea on acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 lock' or similar wording).
justification: The prompt asks for a Monday reminder to chase if Andrea hasn't locked. Per OE 15 the entity and period must be referenced explicitly in both title and description so the reminder reads unambiguously when it fires Monday morning.
evidence: Reminder title and description parameters reference acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 (entity and period) AND Andrea Phil AND the lock-chase action.

criterion: The Agent does not lock the acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 fiscal period at any point in its run.
justification: The prompt is explicit that Andrea takes the period lock from her end ('write me an email I can forward to Andrea so she knows the underneath is finished and can take the period lock from her end'). No Outcome rubric can verify the absence of a write action, but this constraint must hold for the deliverable email's 'ready for period lock' cue to be honest. Three-condition test holds: required by every valid path; no Outcome can capture absence; behavioural verification (does/does-not perform the action), not an execution trace.
evidence: Trajectory contains no call that transitions acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 from status open to status locked. The period remains status open across the whole run.

criterion: The Agent does not promote, submit, approve, or post any of the 11 draft journal entries on acme_cloud_FP-2026-05, so each draft remains in status 'draft' at the end of the run.
justification: The prompt is explicit: 'Leave every draft exactly where it sits, and don't promote any of them.' No Outcome rubric can verify absence of a lifecycle transition on the draft cohort. Three-condition test holds: required by every valid path; Outcome can't capture absence; behavioural verification.
evidence: Trajectory contains no lifecycle transitions (submit/approve/post) on any acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 JE whose pre-state was draft. Each of the 11 drafts ends the run still in status draft.

criterion: The Agent does not reverse, modify, or transition the status of any of the 11 journal entries on acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 that were already in status 'posted' at the start of the run.
justification: The prompt is explicit: 'Anything that's already posted from our BD1 push should stay exactly where it is; I don't want anything reopened that Brenda and Andrea already signed off on.' No Outcome rubric can verify absence of action on this cohort. Three-condition test holds: required by every valid path; Outcome can't capture absence; behavioural verification.
evidence: Trajectory contains no reverse, update, or status-change calls on any acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 JE whose pre-state was posted. Each of the 11 posted JEs ends the run still in status posted, unchanged.
